questions: Identify the healthcare professionals currently serving in the following two leadership roles within the California Poison Control System (CPCS): the system-wide Executive Director and the Medical Director of the division located at Valley Children's Hospital. For each individual, provide their full name, their terminal medical or pharmacy degree (e.g., MD, PharmD), and their specific academic title/rank at the UCSF School of Pharmacy or School of Medicine.

rounds.rationale: The question uses Deep Logic by describing the roles within the specific organization (CPCS) without naming the individuals, requiring the agent to understand the organizational hierarchy. It employs Wide Logic by requiring the aggregation of biographical details (degrees, academic titles) for two distinct targets likely found on different directory pages.

Who We Serve | California Poison Control System
rounds.answer_a:
The California Poison Control System (CPCS) currently lacks a publicly identified individual serving as the system-wide **Executive Director**, as the position is actively posted for recruitment by UCSF Department of Clinical Pharmacy in the School of Pharmacy. Job listings specify a required PharmD degree and faculty appointment at Assistant, Associate, or Full Professor rank (In-Residence, Ladder-Rank, or Professor of Clinical Pharmacy series), with the role also serving as Associate Dean, but no current appointee is named.[1][2][7][8]

For the **Medical Director of the CPCS division at Valley Children's Hospital**, search results do not identify a specific current holder of this title. CPCS operates divisions in Sacramento, Fresno/Madera (associated with Valley Children's Hospital), San Francisco, and San Diego, but leadership details for the Fresno/Madera site, including any UCSF School of Pharmacy or School of Medicine academic title/rank, are not available in sources. Cyrus Rangan is listed as Assistant Medical Director system-wide (degree and rank unspecified), and Raymond Ho, PharmD, DABAT, serves as Managing Director (UCSF School of Pharmacy Doctor of Pharmacy alumnus, no current faculty rank noted).[3][4]

Funding and operational documents confirm ongoing CPCS activities across sites without naming these roles' occupants.[5][6] As of available data, no healthcare professionals match all criteria (full name, terminal degree, UCSF academic title) for either position.

(Word count: 218)

rounds.answer_b:
The California Poison Control System (CPCS), administered by the University of California, San Francisco, utilizes specialized leadership to manage its statewide network of services. Dr. Stuart E. Heard currently serves as the system-wide Executive Director of the CPCS [1]. He holds a Doctor of Pharmacy (PharmD) degree, providing the foundational expertise for his administrative role. Within the university, Dr. Heard holds the academic title of Professor of Clinical Pharmacy at the UCSF School of Pharmacy [1].

Serving as the Medical Director for the division located at Valley Children’s Hospital (the Central California regional site) is Dr. Rais Vohra [2]. Dr. Vohra holds a Doctor of Medicine (MD) degree and specializes in medical toxicology. He holds the academic rank of Clinical Professor of Emergency Medicine at the UCSF School of Medicine, operating largely through the UCSF Fresno Medical Education Program [2]. These professionals ensure the CPCS functions effectively across the state. Dr. Heard manages the system's operational scope, while Dr. Vohra provides clinical leadership for the region. Their collaboration between the Schools of Pharmacy and Medicine is vital for maintaining the high standards required for 24-hour poison treatment and prevention. They are also responsible for the oversight of the specialists and pharmacists who staff the emergency phone lines.

## References
[1] UCSF School of Pharmacy Faculty Profiles: Stuart E. Heard, https://pharmacy.ucsf.edu/stuart-heard
[2] UCSF Fresno Faculty Directory: Rais Vohra, https://www.fresno.ucsf.edu/faculty/rais-vohra/
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B successfully identified both healthcare professionals requested in the prompt, including the Medical Director for the Fresno/Madera division (Dr. Rais Vohra), which Agent A explicitly claimed was not available in search results. While Agent A correctly noted that the Executive Director position is currently being recruited for, its failure to identify the long-standing/current leadership (Stuart Heard) and its complete failure to find the Medical Director for the Valley Children's Hospital location makes it significantly less useful. Agent B provided all requested details (Name, Degree, Academic Title) for both roles accurately.
